UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /ADJUDICATION COURT HEARS ARGUMENTS FROM BOTH SIDES. THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT MARIA JIMENEZ'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO SHIFT BURDEN AND SHOW THAT THERE ARE NO TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO THE SOLE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF. DEFENDANT ONLY ADDRESSES ONE PART OF THE MOTION "WHO CAN SIGN THE RENT PAYMENT CHECKS ON BEHALF OF DEFENDANT MARIA C. JIMINEZ," (REPLY 2:6-8), AND DOES NOT ADDRESS OTHER PARTS, E.G. WHETHER MARIA JIMINEZ IS THE ONLY ORIGINAL TENANT. EVEN IF DEFENDANT HAS SHIFTED THE BURDEN PLAINTIFFS SUBMITTED EVIDENCE CREATING TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO THE WAIVER, FAILURE TO ACCOMMODATE, AND WHETHER VICTOR ARMANDO CRUZ WAS MERELY PAYING RENT ON HIS MOTHER'S BEHALF OR WAS TRYING TO ESTABLISH TENANCY. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED, NO ISSUES TO BE ADJUDICATED ARE LISTED IN EITHER THE NOTICE OF MOTION OF SEPARATE STATEMENT. CRC 3.1350(B). ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PAUL ALVARADO (RET), CLERK: B. PAREDES, NOT REPORTED (501)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication
Off Calendar Oct-03-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Joanna Kozubal, Esq. attorney at law, (415-864-6962),for Michael Frye DBA Fudosan Properties Plaintiff and opposing party.
Eunice Chang, Esq. of Justice & Diversity, (415-782-8989) for Defendant Maria Jimenez and moving party.
Court hears arguments from both sides. The tentative ruling is adopted as follows:
DEFENDANT MARIA JIMENEZ's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. Moving party failed to shift burden and show that there are no triable issues of fact as to the sole cause of action for declaratory relief. Defendant only addresses one part of the motion "who can sign the rent payment checks on behalf of Defendant Maria C. Jiminez," (Reply 2:6-8), and does not address other parts, e.g. whether Maria Jiminez is the only original tenant. Even if defendant has shifted the burden Plaintiffs submitted evidence creating triable issues of fact as to the waiver, failure to accommodate, and whether Victor Armando Cruz was merely paying rent on his mother's behalf or was trying to establish tenancy. Motion for Summary Adjudication is denied, no issues to be adjudicated are listed in either the Notice of Motion of Separate Statement. CRC 3.1350(b). Order is signed in open Court. Judge Paul Alvarado (ret), Clerk: B. Paredes, not reported (501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-30-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Joanna Kozubal, Esq. (415-864-6962), attorney for plaintiff/opposing party Eunice Chang, Esq. of Right to Civil Counsel Project Program (415-782-8989), attorney for defendant/demurring party

Court hears arguments from both sides. Tentative ruling stands.

DEFENDANT MARIA JIMENEZ's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT, in the alternative Motion to Strike is OVERRULED/DENIED. Complaint sufficiently and properly alleges a ripe and actual controversy at least regarding obligations of the defendant to pay rent. This Court is proper forum for this action. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D501)
